title: Prompt Templates for SFT
concept: Formatting instruction data using consistent templates (e.g. '### Instruction: ... ### Response:') teaches the model to recognize where prompts end and answers begin.

exercises.prompt: Why do we format SFT data with consistent prompt templates?

options.text: They teach the model where prompts end and answers begin.
They automatically translate the data into 100 languages.
They make the AI model file size smaller.

exercises.explain: Prompt templates act as guides that show the AI where the user's input ends and where its own response should start.

cards.title: Where Do I Answer?
cards.body: If you feed a model a giant pile of raw chat logs, it gets confused. Where does the human stop talking, and where does the AI start? Prompt templates solve this by wrapping training data in neat, predictable containers.

cards.title: What is a Prompt Template?
cards.body: A prompt template is a standardized structure (like using '### Instruction:' and '### Response:') applied to every single training example. During Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), this teaches the model to recognize where instructions end and responses begin.

cards.title: The Service Dog Signal
cards.body: Imagine training a service dog. If you randomly shout 'sit!', 'please sit down!', or 'would you sit?', the dog gets confused. But if you always use a clear signal, like raised hand + 'SIT', the dog quickly learns exactly what to do. Prompt templates act as that clear, repetitive signal for AI models.

cards.title: Templates in Action
cards.body:
Instead of giving the model raw text, we wrap every training sample like this:

### Instruction:
Summarize this paragraph.
### Input:
[Text here]
### Response:
[Summary here]

By repeating this structure thousands of times, the model learns to stop writing when it sees the response end.

cards.title: Myth: LLMs Can Guess the Structure
cards.body: You might think LLMs are smart enough to figure out where a question ends on their own. In reality, without clear separator tags (like '### Response:'), the model might just continue writing your question instead of answering it!

cards.title: Messy vs. Structured Data
cards.body: Look at how consistent markers organize the text so the AI doesn't get lost.

left.text: Write a poem about rain. Rain drops fall from the sky. What is 2+2? It is 4. (No boundaries!)

right.text: ### Instruction: Write a poem. ### Response: Rain drops fall... ### Instruction: What is 2+2? ### Response: It is 4.

exercises.prompt: What is the best way to format your dataset for Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT)?

options.text: Use a consistent structure like '### Instruction:' and '### Response:' for every example
Mix up different formats randomly to keep the model guessing
Remove all labels and dump raw text files into the trainer
Only use questions and never show the model any answers

exercises.explain: Using '### Instruction:' and '### Response:' consistently sets up a clear pattern that the model learns to follow during training.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Using the exact same prompt template pattern for all training examples helps the AI output cleaner responses.
exercises.explain: Consistent templates are essential because they teach the model the pattern of when to listen (Instruction) and when to talk (Response).

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence about training preparation.
exercises.sentence: To help our AI learn where instructions end and answers begin, we format our training data using consistent prompt _______.
exercises.explain: Applying consistent prompt templates to all training data ensures the AI knows exactly when it is its turn to speak.

exercises.prompt: Which response shows an AI that failed to learn where the prompt ends (due to bad formatting)?

options.text: Prompt: Translate 'hello' to Spanish. Response: Translate 'hello' to Spanish or French or Italian.
Prompt: Translate 'hello' to Spanish. Response: Hola.
Prompt: Translate 'hello' to Spanish. Response: The translation is Hola.

exercises.explain: The first option is generated by an AI that was trained without a structured template, causing it to keep writing the prompt instead of answering it.

exercises.prompt: Why is using a consistent prompt template like '### Instruction: ...' so crucial when fine-tuning a model?

options.text: It makes the AI generate answers much faster
It helps the AI clearly separate user instructions from its own answers
It reduces the size of the AI model on your computer
It translates the training text into different languages automatically

exercises.explain: Without a consistent prompt template during Supervised Fine-Tuning, the AI won't know where instructions end and answers begin, leading to messy, unpredictable outputs in real conversations.
